content: Subject: Short title, extent and commencement.. BNSS Section 1 corresponds to CrPC Section 1. Changes: It contains the Act name, Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023, and provisions regarding the commencement date.

Subject: Definitions.. BNSS Section 2 corresponds to CrPC Section 2. Changes: Several new subsections, such as 2(1)(a), 2(1)(b), 2(1)(d), and 2(1)(e), are added, while others, namely 2(f), 2(k), 2(q), and 2(t) of CrPC, are excluded. Furthermore, certain subsections are modified.
Subject: "audio-video electronic means" .. BNSS Section 2(1)(a) is a new provision with no equivalent in the CrPC. Changes: Newly added.
Subject: "bail". BNSS Section 2(1)(b) is a new provision with no equivalent in the CrPC. Changes: Newly added.
Subject: "bailable offence".. BNSS Section 2(1)(c) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(a)]. Changes: No change.
Subject: "bail bond".. BNSS Section 2(1)(d) is a new provision with no equivalent in the CrPC. Changes: Newly added.
Subject: "bond".. BNSS Section 2(1)(e) is a new provision with no equivalent in the CrPC. Changes: Newly added.
Subject: "charge".. BNSS Section 2(1)(f) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(b)]. Changes: No change.
Subject: "cognizable offence".. BNSS Section 2(1)(g) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(c ). Changes: No change.
Subject: "electronic communication".. BNSS Section 2(1)(i) is a new provision with no equivalent in the CrPC. Changes: Newly added.
Subject: "High Court".. BNSS Section 2(1)(j) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(e ). Changes: No change.
Subject: "inquiry".. BNSS Section 2(1)(k) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(g). Changes: No change.

Subject: "investigation".. BNSS Section 2(1)(l) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(h). Changes: The term "Code" has been substituted with "Sanhita". Explanation that in case of inconsistency provisions of Special Act shall prevail.
Subject: "judicial proceeding".. BNSS Section 2(1)(m ) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(i). Changes: No change.
Subject: "local jurisdiction".. BNSS Section 2(1)(n) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(j). Changes: No change.
Subject: "non-cognizable offence".. BNSS Section 2(1)(o) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(l). Changes: No change.
Subject: "notification".. BNSS Section 2(1)(p) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(m). Changes: No change.
Subject: "offence".. BNSS Section 2(1)(q) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(n). Changes: No change.
Subject: "officer in charge of a police station".. BNSS Section 2(1)(r) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(o). Changes: No change.
Subject: "police report".. BNSS Section 2(1)(t) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(r ). Changes: No change.
Subject: "police station".. BNSS Section 2(1)(u) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(s). Changes: No change.
Subject: "Public Prosecutor".. BNSS Section 2(1)(v) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(u). Changes: No change.
Subject: "sub-division".. BNSS Section 2(1)(w ) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(v). Changes: No change.
Subject: "summons-case".. BNSS Section 2(1)(x) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(w). Changes: No change.

Subject: "victim".. BNSS Section 2(1)(y) corresponds to CrPC Section 2 (wa). Changes: The words "for which accused person has been charged and the expression victim” are replaced by "of the accused person" etc.
Subject: "warrant-case".. BNSS Section 2(1)(z) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(x). Changes: No change.

Subject: Words and expressions used.. BNSS Section 2(2) corresponds to CrPC Section 2(y). Changes: Words and expressions used in Sanhita but not defined here but defined in Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, and in the Information Technology Act, 2000, shall have the meanings respectively assigned to them in that Act and Sanhita.
Subject: Trial of offences under Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and other laws.. BNSS Section 4 corresponds to CrPC Section 4. Changes: No change.
Subject: Saving.. BNSS Section 5 corresponds to CrPC Section 5. Changes: No change.
Subject: Classes of Criminal Courts.. BNSS Section 6 corresponds to CrPC Section 6. Changes: Words "in any Metropolitan area, Metropolitan Magistrates" are excluded.
Subject: Territorial divisions.. BNSS Section 7 corresponds to CrPC Section 7. Changes: Proviso related to Metropolitan area, excluded.
Subject: Courts of Judicial Magistrates.. BNSS Section 9 corresponds to CrPC Section 11. Changes: The words "not being a Metropolitan area" are excluded.
Subject: Chief Judicial Magistrate and Additional Chief Judicial Magistrate, etc.. BNSS Section 10 corresponds to CrPC Section 12. Changes: Ditto.
Subject: Special Judicial Magistrates.. BNSS Section 11 corresponds to CrPC Section 13. Changes: Ditto.
Subject: Local jurisdiction of Judicial Magistrates.. BNSS Section 12 corresponds to CrPC Section 14. Changes: The words "Metropolitan area" and Chief Metropolitan Magistrate are excluded.
Subject: Executive Magistrates.. BNSS Section 14 corresponds to CrPC Section 20. Changes: The words "in any Metropolitan area, Metropolitan Magistrates" are excluded.

Subject: Special Executive Magistrates.. BNSS Section 15 corresponds to CrPC Section 21. Changes: With the new addition, State Government may appoint police officers of the rank Superintendent of Police or e quivalent, as Special Executive Magistrates.
Subject: Local Jurisdiction of Executive Magistrates.. BNSS Section 16 corresponds to CrPC Section 22. Changes: No change.
Subject: Subordination of Executive Magistrates.. BNSS Section 17 corresponds to CrPC Section 23. Changes: The words "other than the additional District Magistrate" are excluded.
Subject: Public Prosecutors.. BNSS Section 18 corresponds to CrPC Section 24. Changes: New proviso is added to subsection (1).

Subject: Assistant Public Prosecutors.. BNSS Section 19 corresponds to CrPC Section 25. Changes: The time line of fourteen days for notice to the state government for the appointment of Assistant Public Prosecutor is mentioned. Proviso is reframed but the meaning is same.

Subject: Directorate of Prosecution.. BNSS Section 20 corresponds to CrPC Section 25A. Changes: The new addition subsection (1) (b) regarding establishment of District Directorate of Prosecution. Subsections (8), (9), (10) and (11) are also newly added etc.
Subject: Courts by which offences are triable.. BNSS Section 21 corresponds to CrPC Section 26. Changes: No change.
Subject: Sentences which High Courts and Sessions Judges may pass.. BNSS Section 22 corresponds to CrPC Section 28. Changes: Sub Section 28(3) of CrPC is excluded.

Subject: Sentences which Magistrates may pass.. BNSS Section 23 corresponds to CrPC Section 29. Changes: Change in amount of fine: ten thousand is replaced by fifty thousand, and five thousand is replaced by ten thousand. The explanation of Section 23 defines "community service." S ub-section 29(4) CrPC is excluded.
Subject: Sentence of imprisonment in default of fine.. BNSS Section 24 corresponds to CrPC Section 30. Changes: No change.

Subject: Sentence in cases of conviction of several offences at one trial.. BNSS Section 25 corresponds to CrPC Section 31. Changes: Sub section (1) is reframed but the essence is same. In subsection (2) (a) fourteen years is replaced by twenty years.
Subject: Mode of conferring powers.. BNSS Section 26 corresponds to CrPC Section 32. Changes: No change.
Subject: Powers of officers appointed.. BNSS Section 27 corresponds to CrPC Section 33. Changes: Ditto.
Subject: Withdrawal of powers.. BNSS Section 28 corresponds to CrPC Section 34. Changes: Ditto.

Subject: Powers of Judges and Magistrates exercisable by their successors-in-office.. BNSS Section 29 corresponds to CrPC Section 35. Changes: The words "Additional or Assistant session Judge" are excluded.
Subject: Powers of superior officers of police.. BNSS Section 30 corresponds to CrPC Section 36. Changes: No change.
Subject: Aid to person, other than police officer, executing warrant.. BNSS Section 32 corresponds to CrPC Section 38. Changes: No change.
Subject: Public to give information of certain offences.. BNSS Section 33 corresponds to CrPC Section 39. Changes: Ditto.

Subject: Duty of officers employed in connection with the affairs of a village to make certain report.. BNSS Section 34 corresponds to CrPC Section 40. Changes: In subsection (2) (ii), instead of writing BNS sections corresponding to IPC sections, the offence punishable for imprisonment of ten years or more, etc., is mentioned. .

Subject: When police may arrest without warrant.. BNSS Section 35 corresponds to CrPC Section 41 & 41A. Changes: A new subsection 7 is added: “No arrest shall be made without prior permission of an officer not below the rank of Deputy Superintendent of Police in case of an offence which is punishable for imprisonment of less than three years and such person is infirm or is above sixty years of age.”
Subject: Procedure of arrest and duties of officer making arrest.. BNSS Section 36 corresponds to CrPC Section 41B. Changes: The words "any other person" is added are clause (c).

Subject: Designated Police Officer.. BNSS Section 37 corresponds to CrPC Section 41C. Changes: Sub-sections (2) and (3) of the CrPC are merged in (1) (b), and provision is given for designating a police officer not below the rank of Assistant Sub-Inspector for the said p urpose.
Subject: Right of arrested person to meet an advocate of his choice during interrogation.. BNSS Section 38 corresponds to CrPC Section 41D. Changes: No change.

Subject: Arrest on refusal to give name and residence.. BNSS Section 39 corresponds to CrPC Section 42. Changes: The words "his executing a bond with or without sureties" are replaced by "bond or bail bond," .

Subject: Arrest by private person and procedure on such arrest.. BNSS Section 40 corresponds to CrPC Section 43. Changes: In subsection (1), without unnecessary delay is further specified by "but within six hours from such arrest." In subsection (2), “re-arrest” is replaced by "take him in custody".
Subject: Arrest by Magistrate.. BNSS Section 41 corresponds to CrPC Section 44. Changes: No change.
Subject: Protection of members of the Armed Forces from arrest.. BNSS Section 42 corresponds to CrPC Section 45. Changes: No change.

Subject: Arrest how made.. BNSS Section 43 corresponds to CrPC Section 46. Changes: A new subsection (3) is added regarding the handcuff of a habitual or repeat offender or accused who has committed certain offences mentioned in the sub-section, etc.
Subject: Search of place entered by person sought to be arrested.. BNSS Section 44 corresponds to CrPC Section 47. Changes: No change.
Subject: Pursuit of offenders into other jurisdictions.. BNSS Section 45 corresponds to CrPC Section 48. Changes: No change.
Subject: Person arrested to be informed of grounds of arrest and of right to bail.. BNSS Section 47 corresponds to CrPC Section 50. Changes: No change.

Subject: Obligation of person making arrest to inform about the arrest, etc , to relative or friend.. BNSS Section 48 corresponds to CrPC Section 50A. Changes: The words "and also to the designated police officer in the district" are added in subsection (1). In subsection (3), the words "may be prescribed in this behalf by" are replaced by "may, by rules, provide".
Subject: Search of arrested person.. BNSS Section 49 corresponds to CrPC Section 51. Changes: No Change except the word "whenever" is excluded from clause (ii) of subsection (1).
Subject: Power to seize offensive weapons.. BNSS Section 50 corresponds to CrPC Section 52. Changes: The words "immediately after the arrest is made" are added.

Subject: Examination of accused by medical practitioner at the request of police officer.. BNSS Section 51 corresponds to CrPC Section 53. Changes: From sub-section (1), "not below the rank of sub-inspector" is excluded, and subsection (3) is newly added regarding the forwarding of examination reports without delay by a medical practitioner to the investigator. In explanation (b), the "defined in clause (h) of Section 2 of the Indian Medical Council Act, 1956 (102 of 1956)" is replaced by "recognised under the National Medical Commission Act, 2019 (30 of 2019)," and "the National Medical Register" is also added as an optional to the State Medical Register.

Subject: Examination of person accused of rape by medical practitioner.. BNSS Section 52 corresponds to CrPC Section 53A. Changes: The words "police officer not below the rank of Sub- Inspector" are replaced by "any police officer".

Subject: Examination of arrested person by medical officer.. BNSS Section 53 corresponds to CrPC Section 54. Changes: A new proviso added to subsection (1) regarding one more examination can be done if it is necessary, in the opinion of the medical practitioner.

Subject: Identification of person arrested.. BNSS Section 54 corresponds to CrPC Section 54A. Changes: The second proviso is excluded as it is already covered under the first proviso by adding the words "the identification process shall be recorded by any audio-video electronic means.".
Subject: Procedure when police officer deputes subordinate to arrest without warrant.. BNSS Section 55 corresponds to CrPC Section 55. Changes: No change.
Subject: Health and safety of arrested person.. BNSS Section 56 corresponds to CrPC Section 55A. Changes: No change.
Subject: Person arrested to be taken before Magistrate or officer in charge of police station.. BNSS Section 57 corresponds to CrPC Section 56. Changes: No change.

Subject: Person arrested not to be detained more than twenty- four hours.. BNSS Section 58 corresponds to CrPC Section 57. Changes: No change except the words "whether having jurisdiction or not" are added.
Subject: Discharge of person apprehended.. BNSS Section 60 corresponds to CrPC Section 59. Changes: "Bail" is replaced with "bail bond".
Subject: Power, on escape, to pursue and retake.. BNSS Section 61 corresponds to CrPC Section 60. Changes: No change.
Subject: Arrest to be made strictly according to the Sanhita.. BNSS Section 62 corresponds to CrPC Section 60A. Changes: No change.

Subject: Form of summons.. BNSS Section 63 corresponds to CrPC Section 61. Changes: A new clause (ii) “in an encrypted or any other form of electronic communication and shall bear the image of the seal of the Court or digital signature” is added.

Subject: Summons how served.. BNSS Section 64 corresponds to CrPC Section 62. Changes: Two new provisos regarding electronic communication, under subsections (1) and (2), are added. The word "personally" is added in sub-section (3).

Subject: Service of summons on corporate bodies, firms, and societies.. BNSS Section 65 corresponds to CrPC Section 63. Changes: In subsection (1), Director and Manager is added. New subsection (2) regarding service of summons on any partner of a firm or other association of individuals.
Subject: Service when persons summoned cannot be found.. BNSS Section 66 corresponds to CrPC Section 64. Changes: The word “male” is excluded to make the provision gender neutral.
Subject: Procedure when service cannot be effected as before provided.. BNSS Section 67 corresponds to CrPC Section 65. Changes: No change.
Subject: Service on Government servant.. BNSS Section 68 corresponds to CrPC Section 66. Changes: No change.
Subject: Service of summons outside local limits.. BNSS Section 69 corresponds to CrPC Section 67. Changes: No change.

Subject: Proof of service in such cases and when serving officer not present.. BNSS Section 70 corresponds to CrPC Section 68. Changes: A new subsection (3) is added, mentioning that service of summons through electronic communication shall be considered duly served, and a copy of such summons shall be attested and kept as proof of service of summons.

Subject: Service of summons on witness by post.. BNSS Section 71 corresponds to CrPC Section 69. Changes: The words "by electronic communication or" are added in subsection (1). In subsection (2) words "or on the proof of delivery of summons under sub-section (3) of section 70 by electronic communication to the satisfaction of the Court," are added.
Subject: Form of warrant of arrest and duration.. BNSS Section 72 corresponds to CrPC Section 70. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power to direct security to be taken.. BNSS Section 73 corresponds to CrPC Section 71. Changes: No change.
Subject: Warrants to whom directed.. BNSS Section 74 corresponds to CrPC Section 72. Changes: No change.
Subject: Warrant may be directed to any person.. BNSS Section 75 corresponds to CrPC Section 73. Changes: No change.
Subject: Notification of substance of warrant.. BNSS Section 77 corresponds to CrPC Section 75. Changes: No change.
Subject: Person arrested to be brought before court without delay.. BNSS Section 78 corresponds to CrPC Section 76. Changes: No change.
Subject: Where warrant may be executed.. BNSS Section 79 corresponds to CrPC Section 77. Changes: No change.
Subject: Warrant forwarded for execution outside jurisdiction.. BNSS Section 80 corresponds to CrPC Section 78. Changes: No change.
Subject: Warrant directed to police officer for execution outside jurisdiction.. BNSS Section 81 corresponds to CrPC Section 79. Changes: No change.

Subject: Procedure on arrest of person against whom warrant issued.. BNSS Section 82 corresponds to CrPC Section 80. Changes: New subsection (2) is added regarding information to designated police officer of the respective district where arrested person resides.
Subject: Procedure by Magistrate before whom such person arrested is brought.. BNSS Section 83 corresponds to CrPC Section 81. Changes: The word "bail" is replaced by "bail bond".

Subject: Proclamation for person absconding.. BNSS Section 84 corresponds to CrPC Section 82. Changes: In subsection (4), IPC sections are replaced by punishments related to corresponding BNS sections. the offence punishable for imprisonment of ten years or more, etc., is mentioned.
Subject: Attachment of property of person absconding.. BNSS Section 85 corresponds to CrPC Section 83. Changes: The words "of property " is added in clause (b) of subsection (1).
Subject: Identification and attachment of property of proclaimed person.. BNSS Section 86 is a new provision with no equivalent in the CrPC. Changes: Newly added.
Subject: Claims and objections to attachment.. BNSS Section 87 corresponds to CrPC Section 84. Changes: No change.
Subject: Release, sale and restoration of attached property.. BNSS Section 88 corresponds to CrPC Section 85. Changes: No change.
Subject: Appeal from order rejecting application for restoration of attached property.. BNSS Section 89 corresponds to CrPC Section 86. Changes: No change.
Subject: Issue of warrant in lieu of, or in addition to, summons.. BNSS Section 90 corresponds to CrPC Section 87. Changes: No change.

Subject: Power to take bond or bail bond for appearance.. BNSS Section 91 corresponds to CrPC Section 88. Changes: The words “or bail bond” are added in heading and words "bond with or without sureties" are replaced by "bond or bail bond".
Subject: Provisions of this Chapter generally applicable to summonses and warrants of arrest.. BNSS Section 93 corresponds to CrPC Section 90. Changes: No change.

Subject: Summons to produce document or other thing.. BNSS Section 94 corresponds to CrPC Section 91. Changes: Regarding the production of documents, the words "electronic communication, including communication devices which is likely to contain digital evidence " are added, and the words "either in physical form or in electronic form, require" are related to summoning a person possessing a document. In subsection (3) (a) corresponding sections of Indian Evidence Act, 1872 are replaced by the sections of the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam, 2023 , word "telegram is excluded.
Subject: Procedure as to letters.. BNSS Section 95 corresponds to CrPC Section 92. Changes: "telegram” and “or telegraph” words are excluded.
Subject: When search-warrant may be issued.. BNSS Section 96 corresponds to CrPC Section 93. Changes: The words “or telegraph” are excluded.

Subject: Search of place suspected to contain stolen property, forged documents, etc.. BNSS Section 97 corresponds to CrPC Section 94. Changes: In clause (a) of subsection (2), "the Metal Token Act, 1889 (1 of 1889)" is replaced by "the Coinage Act, 2011 (11 of 2011)." BNS sections replace the corresponding "IPC" s ections.

Subject: Power to declare certain publications forfeited and to issue search-warrants for the same.. BNSS Section 98 corresponds to CrPC Section 95. Changes: BNS sections replace corresponding IPC Sections.
Subject: Application to High Court to set aside declaration of forfeiture.. BNSS Section 99 corresponds to CrPC Section 96. Changes: No change.
Subject: Search for persons wrongfully confined.. BNSS Section 100 corresponds to CrPC Section 97. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power to compel restoration of abducted females.. BNSS Section 101 corresponds to CrPC Section 98. Changes: The words "under the age of eighteen years" and “husband” are excluded.
Subject: Direction, etc, of search- warrants.. BNSS Section 102 corresponds to CrPC Section 99. Changes: No change.
Subject: Persons in charge of closed place to allow search.. BNSS Section 103 corresponds to CrPC Section 100. Changes: In subsection (8) BNS sections replaces corresponding IPC sections.
Subject: Disposal of things found in search beyond jurisdiction.. BNSS Section 104 corresponds to CrPC Section 101. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power of police officer to seize certain property.. BNSS Section 106 corresponds to CrPC Section 102. Changes: No change.
Subject: Attachment, forfeiture or restoration of property.. BNSS Section 107 is a new provision with no equivalent in the CrPC. Changes: Newly added.
Subject: Magistrate may direct search in his presence.. BNSS Section 108 corresponds to CrPC Section 103. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power to impound document, etc , produced.. BNSS Section 109 corresponds to CrPC Section 104. Changes: No change.
Subject: Reciprocal arrangements regarding processes.. BNSS Section 110 corresponds to CrPC Section 105. Changes: No change.
Subject: Definitions.. BNSS Section 111 corresponds to CrPC Section 105A. Changes: No change.

Subject: Letter of request to competent authority for investigation in a country or place outside India.. BNSS Section 112 corresponds to CrPC Section 166A. Changes: No change except the words "Notwithstanding anything contained in this code" are excluded.

Subject: Letter of request from a country or place outside India to a Court or an authority for investigation in India.. BNSS Section 113 corresponds to CrPC Section 166B. Changes: No change except the words "or such Metropolitan Magistrate" are excluded.

Subject: Assistance in securing transfer of persons.. BNSS Section 114 corresponds to CrPC Section 105B. Changes: No change except the words "Notwithstanding anything contained in this code" are excluded.
Subject: Assistance in relation to orders of attachment or forfeiture of property.. BNSS Section 115 corresponds to CrPC Section 105C. Changes: No change.
Subject: Identifying unlawfully acquired property.. BNSS Section 116 corresponds to CrPC Section 105D. Changes: No change.
Subject: Seizure or attachment of property.. BNSS Section 117 corresponds to CrPC Section 105E. Changes: No change.
Subject: Management of properties seized or forfeited under this Chapter.. BNSS Section 118 corresponds to CrPC Section 105F. Changes: No change.

Subject: Forfeiture of property in certain cases.. BNSS Section 120 corresponds to CrPC Section 105H. Changes: In subsection (4),"the Companies Act, 2013 (18 of 2013) replaces "the Companies Act, 1956 (1 of 1956)".
Subject: Fine in lieu of forfeiture.. BNSS Section 121 corresponds to CrPC Section 105I. Changes: No change.
Subject: Certain transfers to be null and void.. BNSS Section 122 corresponds to CrPC Section 105J. Changes: No change.
Subject: Procedure in respect of letter of request.. BNSS Section 123 corresponds to CrPC Section 105K. Changes: No change.
Subject: Application of this Chapter.. BNSS Section 124 corresponds to CrPC Section 105L. Changes: No change.
Subject: Security for keeping the peace on conviction.. BNSS Section 125 corresponds to CrPC Section 106. Changes: No change.
Subject: Security for keeping the peace in other cases.. BNSS Section 126 corresponds to CrPC Section 107. Changes: No change.
Subject: Security for good behaviour from persons disseminating seditious matters.. BNSS Section 127 corresponds to CrPC Section 108. Changes: BNS sections replace corresponding IPC sections.
Subject: Security for good behaviour from suspected persons.. BNSS Section 128 corresponds to CrPC Section 109. Changes: No change.

Subject: Security for good behaviour from habitual offenders.. BNSS Section 129 corresponds to CrPC Section 110. Changes: BNS sections replace the corresponding IPC sections. Clauses (e) the Protection of Civil Rights Act, 1955; (22 of 1955) and (g) The Food Safety and Standards Act, 2006 (34 of 2006) is added newly under subsection (f) (i). “Bond with sureties” are replaced by “bail bond”.
Subject: Order to be made.. BNSS Section 130 corresponds to CrPC Section 111. Changes: The words "after considering the sufficiency and fitness of sureties" are added.
Subject: Procedure in respect of person present in Court.. BNSS Section 131 corresponds to CrPC Section 112. Changes: No change.
Subject: Summons or warrant in case of person not so present.. BNSS Section 132 corresponds to CrPC Section 113. Changes: No change.
Subject: Copy of order to accompany summons or warrant.. BNSS Section 133 corresponds to CrPC Section 114. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power to dispense with personal attendance.. BNSS Section 134 corresponds to CrPC Section 115. Changes: "pleader" is replaced by "advocate".
Subject: Inquiry as to truth of information.. BNSS Section 135 corresponds to CrPC Section 116. Changes: The words "with or without sureties" are excluded "or bail bond" is added.
Subject: Order to give security.. BNSS Section 136 corresponds to CrPC Section 117. Changes: Ditto.
Subject: Discharge of person informed against.. BNSS Section 137 corresponds to CrPC Section 118. Changes: No change.
Subject: Contents of bond.. BNSS Section 139 corresponds to CrPC Section 120. Changes: "or bail bond" is added.
Subject: Power to reject sureties.. BNSS Section 140 corresponds to CrPC Section 121. Changes: “bond” is replaced by “bail bond”.

Subject: Imprisonment in default of security.. BNSS Section 141 corresponds to CrPC Section 122. Changes: The words "with or without sureties" and "Assistant Sessions Judge" are excluded. "or bail bond" is added.
Subject: Power to release persons imprisoned for failing to give security.. BNSS Section 142 corresponds to CrPC Section 123. Changes: No change.
Subject: Security for unexpired period of bond.. BNSS Section 143 corresponds to CrPC Section 124. Changes: "or bail bond" is added.
Subject: Order for maintenance of wives, children and parents.. BNSS Section 144 corresponds to CrPC Section 125. Changes: "minor" is replaced by "child".
Subject: Procedure.. BNSS Section 145 corresponds to CrPC Section 126. Changes: In subsection (1), clause (d) is added. In subsection (2), pleader is replaced by "advocate".
Subject: Alteration in allowance.. BNSS Section 146 corresponds to CrPC Section 127. Changes: No change.
Subject: Enforcement of order of maintenance.. BNSS Section 147 corresponds to CrPC Section 128. Changes: No change.
Subject: Dispersal of assembly by use of civil force.. BNSS Section 148 corresponds to CrPC Section 129. Changes: No change.

Subject: Use of armed forces to disperse assembly.. BNSS Section 149 corresponds to CrPC Section 130. Changes: In subsection (1), the words "any such assembly" are replaced by "any assembly referred to in sub-section (1) of section 148," and "Executive Magistrate of the highest rank" is replaced by "District Magistrate or any other Executive Magistrate authorised by him". In Subsection 2, "Magistrate" is replaced by "Executive Magistrate".
Subject: Power of certain armed force officers to disperse assembly.. BNSS Section 150 corresponds to CrPC Section 131. Changes: No change.
Subject: Protection against prosecution for acts done under sections 148, 149 and 150.. BNSS Section 151 corresponds to CrPC Section 132. Changes: The word "military" is replaced by "army".
Subject: Conditional order for removal of nuisance.. BNSS Section 152 corresponds to CrPC Section 133. Changes: No change.
Subject: Service or notification of order.. BNSS Section 153 corresponds to CrPC Section 134. Changes: No change.

Subject: Person to whom order is addressed to obey or show cause.. BNSS Section 154 corresponds to CrPC Section 135. Changes: In clause (b) the words "and such appearance or hearing may be permitted through audio-video conferencing." are added.
Subject: Procedure where existence of public right is denied.. BNSS Section 156 corresponds to CrPC Section 137. Changes: No change.

Subject: Procedure where person against whom order is made under section 152 appears to show cause.. BNSS Section 157 corresponds to CrPC Section 138. Changes: New proviso regarding completion of proceedings is added thereby a timeline is stipulated for the purpose.
Subject: Power of Magistrate to direct local investigation and examination of an expert.. BNSS Section 158 corresponds to CrPC Section 139. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power of Magistrate to furnish written instructions, etc.. BNSS Section 159 corresponds to CrPC Section 140. Changes: No change.
Subject: Procedure on order being made absolute and consequences of disobedience.. BNSS Section 160 corresponds to CrPC Section 141. Changes: No change.
Subject: Injunction pending inquiry.. BNSS Section 161 corresponds to CrPC Section 142. Changes: No change.
Subject: Magistrate may prohibit repetition or continuance of public nuisance.. BNSS Section 162 corresponds to CrPC Section 143. Changes: The words "or Deputy Commissioner of Police" are added.
Subject: Power to issue order in urgent cases of nuisance or apprehended danger.. BNSS Section 163 corresponds to CrPC Section 144. Changes: No change.
Subject: Procedure where dispute concerning land or water is likely to cause breach of peace.. BNSS Section 164 corresponds to CrPC Section 145. Changes: No change.

Subject: Dispute concerning right of use of land or water.. BNSS Section 166 corresponds to CrPC Section 147. Changes: In Explanation the words "For the purposes of this sub- section" are added. The word “pleader” is replaced by “advocate”.
Subject: Local inquiry.. BNSS Section 167 corresponds to CrPC Section 148. Changes: The word “pleader” is replaced by “advocate”.
Subject: Police to prevent cognizable offences.. BNSS Section 168 corresponds to CrPC Section 149. Changes: No change.
Subject: Information of design to commit cognizable offences.. BNSS Section 169 corresponds to CrPC Section 150. Changes: No change.
Subject: Prevention of injury to public property.. BNSS Section 171 corresponds to CrPC Section 152. Changes: No change.
Subject: Persons bound to conform to lawful directions of police.. BNSS Section 172 is a new provision with no equivalent in the CrPC.

Subject: Information in cognizable cases.. BNSS Section 173 corresponds to CrPC Section 154. Changes: By adding the words "irrespective of the area where the offence is committed”, the scope is expanded to lodge an FIR. This corresponds to the concept of ‘zero FIR’By adding the word "by electronic communication," thereby it is made contemporary. Clause (ii) is added in subsection (1); this is known as eFIR.Subsection (3) is newly added regarding preliminary inquiry to ascertain whether there exists a prima facie case and proceed for investigation on the prima facie case for the cognizable offences which are made punishable for three years or more but less than seven years".In subsection (4), the words "failing which such aggrieved person may make an application to the Magistrate." are added.

Subject: Information as to non- cognizable cases and investigation of such cases.. BNSS Section 174 corresponds to CrPC Section 155. Changes: Clause (ii) in subsection (1) is added regarding forwarding the daily diary report of non-cognizable cases fortnightly to the Magistrate.

Subject: Police officer's power to investigate cognizable case.. BNSS Section 175 corresponds to CrPC Section 156. Changes: A new proviso to subsection (1) is added. Sub-section (4) is newly added to protect public servants in the course of discharge of public duty against undue harassment, and subsection (3) is also amended according to sub-section(4) of section 173.

Subject: Procedure for investigation.. BNSS Section 176 corresponds to CrPC Section 157. Changes: In clause (b) of subsection (1), audio-video electronic means are added. In subsection (2), the words, "forward the daily diary report fortnightly to the Magistrate" are added. Sub-section (3) is newly added, which stipulates forensic experts visit the crime scene as mandatory for offences punishable for seven years or more.
Subject: Report how submitted.. BNSS Section 177 corresponds to CrPC Section 158. Changes: No change.

Subject: Police officer's power to require attendance of witnesses.. BNSS Section 179 corresponds to CrPC Section 160. Changes: In the first proviso, "sixty-five years" is replaced by "sixty years" and the words "or a person with acute illness" are added. One new proviso is added. Thus, the scope of the provision is expanded.
Subject: Examination of witnesses by police.. BNSS Section 180 corresponds to CrPC Section 161. Changes: No change.

Subject: Statements to police and use thereof.. BNSS Section 181 corresponds to CrPC Section 162. Changes: Heading of the section is changed. BSA sections replace corresponding Indian Evidence Act sections.
Subject: No inducement to be offered.. BNSS Section 182 corresponds to CrPC Section 163. Changes: No change.

Subject: Recording of confessions and statements.. BNSS Section 183 corresponds to CrPC Section 164. Changes: The newly added first proviso to Section 183(6)(a) provides that statements are to be recorded by a woman Magistrate as far as practicable.A newly added second proviso to Section 183(6)(a) provides for mandatory recording of a statement of witness by Magistrate relating to the offences punishable with imprisonment for ten years or more, imprisonment for life, or with death.The fourth proviso to Section 183(6)(a) provides for the recording of the statement of a temporarily or permanently mentally or physically disabled person through audio-video electronic means.

Subject: Medical examination of the victim of rape.. BNSS Section 184 corresponds to CrPC Section 164A. Changes: "without delay" is replaced by "Within a period of seven days" for medical practitioner to forward the report to Investigating officer.
Subject: When officer in charge of police station may require another to issue search- warrant.. BNSS Section 186 corresponds to CrPC Section 166. Changes: No change.
Subject: Report of investigation by subordinate police officer.. BNSS Section 188 corresponds to CrPC Section 168. Changes: No change.
Subject: Release of accused when evidence deficient.. BNSS Section 189 corresponds to CrPC Section 169. Changes: The words "with or without sureties" are excluded and "or bail bond" are added.

Subject: Cases to be sent to Magistrate, when evidence is sufficient.. BNSS Section 190 corresponds to CrPC Section 170. Changes: New proviso is added to subsection (1) regarding taking security from accused not in custody before the magistrate and mandate the Magistrate to accept the same.
Subject: Diary of proceedings in investigation.. BNSS Section 192 corresponds to CrPC Section 172. Changes: No change.

Subject: Police to enquire and report on suicide, etc.. BNSS Section 194 corresponds to CrPC Section 174. Changes: In subsection (2) "forthwith" is replaced by "within twenty- four hours" for sending the report to DM and SDM. The word “man” is replaced by “person”.

Subject: Power to summon persons.. BNSS Section 195 corresponds to CrPC Section 175. Changes: Proviso to Subsection (1) of 195 provides immunity to male persons under the age of fifteen years or above the age of sixty years, or a woman or a mentally or physically disabled persons or a person with acute illness shall not be required to attend at place other than the place where such person resides. However, they can attend at police station willingly.

Subject: Inquiry by Magistrate into cause of death. BNSS Section 196 corresponds to CrPC Section 176. Changes: The words "Judicial Magistrate" are replaced by "Magistrate", and Metropolitan Magistrate is excluded.
Subject: Ordinary place of inquiry and trial.. BNSS Section 197 corresponds to CrPC Section 177. Changes: No change.
Subject: Place of inquiry or trial.. BNSS Section 198 corresponds to CrPC Section 178. Changes: No change.
Subject: Offence triable where act is done or consequence ensues.. BNSS Section 199 corresponds to CrPC Section 179. Changes: No change.
Subject: Place of trial where act is an offence by reason of relation to other offence. BNSS Section 200 corresponds to CrPC Section 180. Changes: No change.

Subject: Place of trial in case of certain offences.. BNSS Section 201 corresponds to CrPC Section 181. Changes: The words "of being a thug, or murder committed by a thug" are excluded as definition of Thug and punishment being one are also excluded from the BNS.

Subject: Offences committed by means of electronic communications, letters, etc.. BNSS Section 202 corresponds to CrPC Section 182. Changes: The words "electronic communications" added. BNS sections replace corresponding IPC sections.
Subject: Place of trial for offences triable together.. BNSS Section 204 corresponds to CrPC Section 184. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power to order cases to be tried in different sessions divisions.. BNSS Section 205 corresponds to CrPC Section 185. Changes: No change.
Subject: High Court to decide, in case of doubt, district where inquiry or trial shall take place.. BNSS Section 206 corresponds to CrPC Section 186. Changes: No change.

Subject: Power to issue summons or warrant for offence committed beyond local jurisdiction.. BNSS Section 207 corresponds to CrPC Section 187. Changes: The words "with or without sureties" are replaced by "or bail bond".
Subject: Offence committed outside India.. BNSS Section 208 corresponds to CrPC Section 188. Changes: The words "or where the offence is registered in India" are added.

Subject: Receipt of evidence relating to offences committed outside India.. BNSS Section 209 corresponds to CrPC Section 189. Changes: The words "either in physical form or in electronic form" are added.

Subject: Cognizance of offences by Magistrates.. BNSS Section 210 corresponds to CrPC Section 190. Changes: The words "including any complaint filed by a person authorised under any special law" and "submitted in any mode including electronic mode " are added in clause (a ) and (b ) of subsection (1).
Subject: Transfer on application of the accused.. BNSS Section 211 corresponds to CrPC Section 191. Changes: No change.
Subject: Making over of cases to Magistrates.. BNSS Section 212 corresponds to CrPC Section 192. Changes: No change.
Subject: Cognizance of offences by Courts of Session.. BNSS Section 213 corresponds to CrPC Section 193. Changes: No change.
Subject: Additional Sessions Judges to try cases made over to them.. BNSS Section 214 corresponds to CrPC Section 194. Changes: No change except words "or Assistant sessions Judge" are excluded.

Subject: Prosecution for contempt of lawful authority of public servants, for offences against public justice and for offences relating to documents given in evidence.. BNSS Section 215 corresponds to CrPC Section 195. Changes: BNS sections replace corresponding IPC sections. The words "or of some other public servant who is authorised by the concerned public servant so to do" are added.
Subject: Procedure for witnesses in case of threatening, etc.. BNSS Section 216 corresponds to CrPC Section 195A. Changes: No change.

Subject: Prosecution of Judges and public servants.. BNSS Section 218 corresponds to CrPC Section 197. Changes: The BNS sections replace the corresponding IPC sections. A second proviso to 218(1)(b) is added, which provides that such Government shall take a decision within a period of one hundred and twenty days from the date of receipt of the request for sanction, and in case it fails to do so, the sanction shall be deemed to have been accorded by such Government. This will curb the tendency to help an accused p ublic servant by such delay tactics.

Subject: Prosecution for offences against marriage.. BNSS Section 219 corresponds to CrPC Section 198. Changes: The Word "child" replaces "minor" and "child" also replaces "person under the age of 18 years", "is an idiot, or a lunatic" is replaced by "is of unsound mind or is having intellectual disability requiring higher support needs".
Subject: Prosecution of offences under section 498A of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023.. BNSS Section 220 corresponds to CrPC Section 198A. Changes: No change.
Subject: Cognizance of offence. BNSS Section 221 corresponds to CrPC Section 198B. Changes: No change.

Subject: Prosecution for defamation.. BNSS Section 222 corresponds to CrPC Section 199. Changes: BNS Chapters and sections replace corresponding IPC Chapters and sections. "child" replaces "under the age of eighteen years " or is of unsound mind replaces "or is having intellectual disability or is an idiot or a lunatic".

Subject: Examination of complainant.. BNSS Section 223 corresponds to CrPC Section 200. Changes: The newly added first proviso to Section 223(1) provides that no cognizance of an offence shall be taken by the Magistrate without giving the accused an opportunity of being heard. A new sub-section (2) of Section 223 provides that a Magistrate shall not take cognizance on a complaint against a public servant for any offence alleged to have been committed in the course of the discharge of his official functions or duties unless—(a) such public servant is given an opportunity to make assertions as to the situation that led to the incident so alleged; and (b) a report containing facts and circumstances of the incident from the officer superior to such a public servant is r eceived.
Subject: Procedure by Magistrate not competent to take cognizance of the case.. BNSS Section 224 corresponds to CrPC Section 201. Changes: No change.
Subject: Dismissal of complaint.. BNSS Section 226 corresponds to CrPC Section 203. Changes: No change.

Subject: Issue of process.. BNSS Section 227 corresponds to CrPC Section 204. Changes: The new proviso to sub-section (1) of Section 227 provides that summons or warrant may also be issued through electronic means.
Subject: Magistrate may dispense with personal attendance of accused.. BNSS Section 228 corresponds to CrPC Section 205. Changes: "pleader" is replaced by "advocate".

Subject: Special summons in cases of petty offence.. BNSS Section 229 corresponds to CrPC Section 206. Changes: "pleader" is replaced by "advocate." The threshold amount of fine for the purpose of the definition of "petty offence" is increased by this section from one thousand rupees to five thousand rupees. The monetary limit of the fine specified in summons is increased from one thousand rupees to five thousand rupees. “the Motor Vehicles Act, 1939 (59 of 1939)” is replaced by “the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988 (59 of 1988).”.

Subject: Supply to the accused of copy of police report and other documents.. BNSS Section 230 corresponds to CrPC Section 207. Changes: This section states that the documents are to be supplied free of cost to the accused without any delay, and in no case beyond 14 days from the date of production or appearance of the accused, and should also be provided to the victim if represented by an advocate. The last proviso states that the supply of documents in electronic form shall be considered as duly furnished.

Subject: Supply of copies of statements and documents to accused in other cases triable by Court of Session.. BNSS Section 231 corresponds to CrPC Section 208. Changes: Last proviso states that supply of documents to accused in electronic form shall be considered as duly furnished. "pleader" is replaced by "advocate".

Subject: Commitment of case to Court of Session when offence is triable exclusively by it.. BNSS Section 232 corresponds to CrPC Section 209. Changes: Two new provisos are added herein. By first proviso proceedings have been made time-bound. The proceedings under this section shall be completed within a period of ninety days from the date of taking cognizance, and such period may be extended, by the Magistrate for a period not exceeding one hundred eighty days for the reasons to be recorded in writing. Second proviso states that any application filed before the Magistrate by the accused or the victim or any person authorised by such person in a case triable by Court of Session, shall be forwarded to the Court of Session with the committal of the case.
Subject: Contents of charge.. BNSS Section 234 corresponds to CrPC Section 211. Changes: No change.
Subject: Particulars as to time, place and person.. BNSS Section 235 corresponds to CrPC Section 212. Changes: No change.
Subject: When manner of committing offence must be stated.. BNSS Section 236 corresponds to CrPC Section 213. Changes: No change.
Subject: Words in charge taken in sense of law under which offence is punishable.. BNSS Section 237 corresponds to CrPC Section 214. Changes: No change.
Subject: Effect of errors.. BNSS Section 238 corresponds to CrPC Section 215. Changes: No change.
Subject: Court may alter charge.. BNSS Section 239 corresponds to CrPC Section 216. Changes: No change.
Subject: Recall of witnesses when charge altered.. BNSS Section 240 corresponds to CrPC Section 217. Changes: No change.
Subject: Separate charges for distinct offences.. BNSS Section 241 corresponds to CrPC Section 218. Changes: No change.
Subject: Offences of same kind within year may be charged together.. BNSS Section 242 corresponds to CrPC Section 219. Changes: No change.

Subject: Trial for more than one offence.. BNSS Section 243 corresponds to CrPC Section 220. Changes: Subsection 243(1) reorganises illustrations (b) and (c) given in CrPC, citing "adultery" and "enticing away," which are r eplaced by "rape" in the rephrased illustration (b) herein.
Subject: Where it is doubtful what offence has been committed.. BNSS Section 244 corresponds to CrPC Section 221. Changes: No change.
Subject: When offence proved included in offence charged.. BNSS Section 245 corresponds to CrPC Section 222. Changes: No change.
Subject: What persons may be charged jointly.. BNSS Section 246 corresponds to CrPC Section 223. Changes: No change.
Subject: Withdrawal of remaining charges on conviction on one of several charges.. BNSS Section 247 corresponds to CrPC Section 224. Changes: No change.
Subject: Trial to be conducted by Public Prosecutor.. BNSS Section 248 corresponds to CrPC Section 225. Changes: No change.
Subject: Opening case for prosecution.. BNSS Section 249 corresponds to CrPC Section 226. Changes: The words "or under any other law for the time being in force" are added.

Subject: Discharge.. BNSS Section 250 corresponds to CrPC Section 227. Changes: New subsection (1) provides that the accused may prefer an application for discharge within a period of sixty days from the date of commencement of the case under Section 232.
Subject: Conviction on plea of guilty.. BNSS Section 252 corresponds to CrPC Section 229. Changes: No change.
Subject: Date for prosecution evidence.. BNSS Section 253 corresponds to CrPC Section 230. Changes: No change.
Subject: Acquittal.. BNSS Section 255 corresponds to CrPC Section 232. Changes: No change.
Subject: Entering upon defence.. BNSS Section 256 corresponds to CrPC Section 233. Changes: No change.
Subject: Arguments.. BNSS Section 257 corresponds to CrPC Section 234. Changes: "pleader" is replaced by "advocate".

Subject: Judgment of acquittal or conviction.. BNSS Section 258 corresponds to CrPC Section 235. Changes: New addition to subsection (1) for time bound disposal- After hearing arguments and points of law (if any), the Judge shall give a judgement in the case, “as soon as possible, within a period of thirty days from the date of completion of arguments, which may be extended, to a period of fortyfive days for reasons to be recorded in writing”.
Subject: Previous conviction.. BNSS Section 259 corresponds to CrPC Section 236. Changes: No change.

Subject: Procedure in cases instituted under sib-section 2 of section 222.. BNSS Section 260 corresponds to CrPC Section 237. Changes: Upper limit of compensation amount increased from one thousand rupees to five thousand rupees in sub-section (4).
Subject: Compliance with section 230. BNSS Section 261 corresponds to CrPC Section 238. Changes: No change.

Subject: When accused shall be discharged.. BNSS Section 262 corresponds to CrPC Section 239. Changes: New subsection 262(1) provides the time limit of application for discharge by accused as "within a period of sixty days from the date of supply of copies of document under section 230". Examination of accused through audio- video electronic means can be done as added in sub-section (2).

Subject: Framing of charge.. BNSS Section 263 corresponds to CrPC Section 240. Changes: Time limit for framing of charge against the accused is added within a period of sixty days from the date of first hearing on charge.
Subject: Conviction on plea of guilty.. BNSS Section 264 corresponds to CrPC Section 241. Changes: No change.

Subject: Evidence for defence.. BNSS Section 266 corresponds to CrPC Section 243. Changes: New second proviso to Section 266(2) provides that the examination of witnesses may be done by audio-video electronic means at the designated place to be notified by t he State Government.
Subject: Evidence for prosecution.. BNSS Section 267 corresponds to CrPC Section 244. Changes: No change.
Subject: When accused shall be discharged.. BNSS Section 268 corresponds to CrPC Section 245. Changes: No change.

Subject: Procedure where accused is not discharged.. BNSS Section 269 corresponds to CrPC Section 246. Changes: The newly added subsection (7) provides that if the attendance of the prosecution witnesses under sub-sections (5) and (6) cannot be secured for cross-examination, it shall be deemed that such witness has not been examined for not b eing available and Magistrate proceed further.
Subject: Evidence for defence.. BNSS Section 270 corresponds to CrPC Section 247. Changes: No change.
Subject: Acquittal or conviction.. BNSS Section 271 corresponds to CrPC Section 248. Changes: No change.

Subject: Absence of complainant.. BNSS Section 272 corresponds to CrPC Section 249. Changes: It is added that the Magistrate may “after giving thirty days’ time to the complainant to be present before proceeding further.

Subject: Compensation for accusation without reasonable cause.. BNSS Section 273 corresponds to CrPC Section 250. Changes: BNS Sections replace corresponding IPC sections. Compensation amount under sub-section (6) increased from "one hundred rupees" to “two thousand rupees”.

Subject: Substance of accusation to be stated.. BNSS Section 274 corresponds to CrPC Section 251. Changes: The new proviso to Section 274 provides that if the Magistrate considers the accusation groundless, he shall, after recording reasons in writing, release the accused, and s uch release shall have the effect of discharge.
Subject: Conviction on plea of guilty.. BNSS Section 275 corresponds to CrPC Section 252. Changes: No change.
Subject: Conviction on plea of guilty in absence of accused in petty cases.. BNSS Section 276 corresponds to CrPC Section 253. Changes: "pleader" is replaced by "advocate".
Subject: Procedure when not convicted.. BNSS Section 277 corresponds to CrPC Section 254. Changes: No change.
Subject: Acquittal or conviction.. BNSS Section 278 corresponds to CrPC Section 255. Changes: No change.

Subject: Non-appearance or death of complainant.. BNSS Section 279 corresponds to CrPC Section 256. Changes: The words "after giving thirty days' time to the complainant to be present" are added. "pleader" is replaced by "advocate".
Subject: Withdrawal of complaint.. BNSS Section 280 corresponds to CrPC Section 257. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power to stop proceedings in certain cases.. BNSS Section 281 corresponds to CrPC Section 258. Changes: No change.
Subject: Judgment in cases tried summarily.. BNSS Section 287 corresponds to CrPC Section 264. Changes: No change.
Subject: Language of record and judgment.. BNSS Section 288 corresponds to CrPC Section 265. Changes: No change.
Subject: Application of the Chapter.. BNSS Section 289 corresponds to CrPC Section 265A. Changes: "Below the age of fourteen years" excluded from 289(1)(b), thereby expanding the scope.

Subject: Application for plea bargaining.. BNSS Section 290 corresponds to CrPC Section 265B. Changes: Time limit for plea bargaining "within a period of thirty days from the date of framing of charge" is added in subsection (1). 290 (4) (a) "not exceeding sixty days" is added, regarding accused to work out a mutually satisfactory disposition of the case". The words “as the case may be” excluded.
Subject: Guidelines for mutually satisfactory disposition.. BNSS Section 291 corresponds to CrPC Section 265C. Changes: "pleader" is replaced by "advocate".
Subject: Report of the mutually satisfactory disposition to be submitted before the Court.. BNSS Section 292 corresponds to CrPC Section 265D. Changes: No change.

Subject: Disposal of the case.. BNSS Section 293 corresponds to CrPC Section 265E. Changes: Section 293 clauses (c ) and (d) make changes to require the court to show leniency towards first -time offenders who opt for plea bargaining. They may be sentenced to one fourth of minimum punishment instead of half and one- nineth instead of one-fourth respectively.
Subject: Judgment of the Court.. BNSS Section 294 corresponds to CrPC Section 265F. Changes: No change.
Subject: Finality of the judgment.. BNSS Section 295 corresponds to CrPC Section 265G. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power of the Court in plea bargaining.. BNSS Section 296 corresponds to CrPC Section 265H. Changes: No change.
Subject: Savings.. BNSS Section 298 corresponds to CrPC Section 265J. Changes: No change.
Subject: Statements of accused not to be used.. BNSS Section 299 corresponds to CrPC Section 265K. Changes: No change.
Subject: Non-application of the Chapter.. BNSS Section 300 corresponds to CrPC Section 265L. Changes: No change.
Subject: Definitions.. BNSS Section 301 corresponds to CrPC Section 266. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power to require attendance of prisoners.. BNSS Section 302 corresponds to CrPC Section 267. Changes: The words “as the case may be” are excluded.

Subject: Power of State Government or Central Government to exclude certain persons from operation of section 302.. BNSS Section 303 corresponds to CrPC Section 268. Changes: "or Central Government" is added in the heading and other places. "the Central Government in the cases instituted by its central agency, as the case may be" are added in subsection (2).
Subject: Officer in charge of prison to abstain from carrying out order in certain contingencies.. BNSS Section 304 corresponds to CrPC Section 269. Changes: No change.
Subject: Prisoner to be brought to Court in custody.. BNSS Section 305 corresponds to CrPC Section 270. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power to issue commission for examination of witness in prison.. BNSS Section 306 corresponds to CrPC Section 271. Changes: No change.
Subject: Language of Courts.. BNSS Section 307 corresponds to CrPC Section 272. Changes: No change.

Subject: Evidence to be taken in presence of accused.. BNSS Section 308 corresponds to CrPC Section 273. Changes: The words "through audio-video electronic means at the designated place to be notified by the State Government" are added.
Subject: Record in summons-cases and inquiries.. BNSS Section 309 corresponds to CrPC Section 274. Changes: No change.
Subject: Record in warrant-cases.. BNSS Section 310 corresponds to CrPC Section 275. Changes: No change.
Subject: Record in trial before Court of Session.. BNSS Section 311 corresponds to CrPC Section 276. Changes: No change.
Subject: Language of record of evidence.. BNSS Section 312 corresponds to CrPC Section 277. Changes: No change.
Subject: Procedure in regard to such evidence when completed.. BNSS Section 313 corresponds to CrPC Section 278. Changes: The word "pleader" is replaced by "advocate" in heading and other places.
Subject: Interpretation of evidence to accused or his pleader.. BNSS Section 314 corresponds to CrPC Section 279. Changes: Ditto.
Subject: Remarks respecting demeanour of witness.. BNSS Section 315 corresponds to CrPC Section 280. Changes: No change.
Subject: Interpreter to be bound to interpret truthfully.. BNSS Section 317 corresponds to CrPC Section 282. Changes: No change.
Subject: Record in High Court.. BNSS Section 318 corresponds to CrPC Section 283. Changes: No change.
Subject: When attendance of witness may be dispensed with and commission issued.. BNSS Section 319 corresponds to CrPC Section 284. Changes: "pleader" is replaced by "advocate".
Subject: Commission to whom to be issued.. BNSS Section 320 corresponds to CrPC Section 285. Changes: “Chief Metropolitan Magistrate" excluded.

Subject: Execution of commissions.. BNSS Section 321 corresponds to CrPC Section 286. Changes: "Chief Metropolitan Magistrate or Chief Judicial Magistrate, or such Metropolitan or Judicial Magistrate" is replaced by "Chief Judicial Magistrate or such Magistrate".
Subject: Parties may examine witnesses.. BNSS Section 322 corresponds to CrPC Section 287. Changes: The word “pleader” is replaced by “advocate”. “as the case may be” excluded.
Subject: Return of commission.. BNSS Section 323 corresponds to CrPC Section 288. Changes: BSA sections replace corresponding Indian Evidence Act sections.
Subject: Adjournment of proceeding.. BNSS Section 324 corresponds to CrPC Section 289. Changes: No change.
Subject: Execution of foreign commissions.. BNSS Section 325 corresponds to CrPC Section 290. Changes: No change.
Subject: Deposition of medical witness.. BNSS Section 326 corresponds to CrPC Section 291. Changes: No change.
Subject: Identification report of Magistrate.. BNSS Section 327 corresponds to CrPC Section 291A. Changes: BSA sections replace corresponding Indian Evidence Act sections.

Subject: Evidence of officers of the Mint.. BNSS Section 328 corresponds to CrPC Section 292. Changes: BSA sections replace corresponding Indian Evidence Act sections. “any such”, “of the mint officer” and “as the case may be” are excluded.

Subject: Reports of certain Government scientific experts.. BNSS Section 329 corresponds to CrPC Section 293. Changes: In 329(4) (g) the words "State Government or, and or certified "are added and, “Government” excluded.

Subject: No formal proof of certain documents.. BNSS Section 330 corresponds to CrPC Section 294. Changes: Two new provisos are added. Time limit of thirty days for acceptance or denial of the genuineness of the document, provided the Court may relax the time limit as stated in the first proviso. And the second proviso states that experts should be called only if he is disputed by any parties during the trial. The words “may be prescribed by the State Government” is replaced by “the State Government may, by rules, provide”
Subject: Evidence of formal character on affidavit.. BNSS Section 332 corresponds to CrPC Section 296. Changes: No change.
Subject: Authorities before whom affidavits may be sworn.. BNSS Section 333 corresponds to CrPC Section 297. Changes: No change.
Subject: Previous conviction or acquittal how proved.. BNSS Section 334 corresponds to CrPC Section 298. Changes: No change.
Subject: Record of evidence in absence of accused.. BNSS Section 335 corresponds to CrPC Section 299. Changes: No change.

Subject: Evidence of public servants, experts, police officers in certain cases.. BNSS Section 336 is a new provision with no equivalent in the CrPC. Changes: Newly added section. Evidence of public servants, experts, police officers in certain cases may be secured through the successor officer of such a public servant, etc.
Subject: Person once convicted or acquitted not to be tried for same offence.. BNSS Section 337 corresponds to CrPC Section 300. Changes: No change.
Subject: Appearance by Public Prosecutors.. BNSS Section 338 corresponds to CrPC Section 301. Changes: "Pleader" is replaced by "Advocate".
Subject: Permission to conduct prosecution.. BNSS Section 339 corresponds to CrPC Section 302. Changes: Ditto.
Subject: Right of person against whom proceedings are instituted to be defended.. BNSS Section 340 corresponds to CrPC Section 303. Changes: Ditto.
Subject: Legal aid to accused at State expense in certain cases.. BNSS Section 341 corresponds to CrPC Section 304. Changes: Ditto.
Subject: Procedure when corporation or registered society is an accused.. BNSS Section 342 corresponds to CrPC Section 305. Changes: The words "duly authorised by him" are added in subsection (5).

Subject: Tender of pardon to accomplice.. BNSS Section 343 corresponds to CrPC Section 306. Changes: "or a Metropolitan magistrate" excluded and "the criminal law amendment Act 1952" replaced by "any other law for the time being in force".
Subject: Power to direct tender of pardon.. BNSS Section 344 corresponds to CrPC Section 307. Changes: No change.
Subject: Trial of person not complying with conditions of pardon.. BNSS Section 345 corresponds to CrPC Section 308. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power to postpone or adjourn proceedings.. BNSS Section 346 corresponds to CrPC Section 309.
Subject: Local inspection.. BNSS Section 347 corresponds to CrPC Section 310. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power to summon material witness, or examine person present.. BNSS Section 348 corresponds to CrPC Section 311. Changes: No change.

Subject: Power of Magistrate to order person to give specimen signatures or handwriting.. BNSS Section 349 corresponds to CrPC Section 311A. Changes: The words "or finger impressions" and “or voice sample" are added. One new proviso added which provides that the Magistrate may, for reasons to be recorded in writing, order any person to give such a specimen or sample without him b eing arrested.
Subject: Expenses of complainants and witnesses.. BNSS Section 350 corresponds to CrPC Section 312. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power to examine the accused.. BNSS Section 351 corresponds to CrPC Section 313. Changes: No change.
Subject: Oral arguments and memorandum of arguments.. BNSS Section 352 corresponds to CrPC Section 314. Changes: No change.
Subject: Accused person to be competent witness.. BNSS Section 353 corresponds to CrPC Section 315. Changes: No change.
Subject: No influence to be used to induce disclosure.. BNSS Section 354 corresponds to CrPC Section 316. Changes: No change.

Subject: Provision for inquiries and trial being held in the absence of accused in certain cases.. BNSS Section 355 corresponds to CrPC Section 317. Changes: "pleader" is replaced by "advocate". A new explanation is added –“For the purpose of this section, personal attendance of the accused includes attendance through audio-video electronic means”.

Subject: Inquiry, trial or judgment in absentia of proclaimed offender.. BNSS Section 356 is a new provision with no equivalent in the CrPC. Changes: A newly added section which gives provision for Inquiry, trial or judgment in absentia of proclaimed offender. An extra-ordinary provision.
Subject: Procedure where accused does not understand proceedings.. BNSS Section 357 corresponds to CrPC Section 318. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power to proceed against other persons appearing to be guilty of offence.. BNSS Section 358 corresponds to CrPC Section 319. Changes: No change.

Subject: Compounding of offences.. BNSS Section 359 corresponds to CrPC Section 320. Changes: In the table of compoundable offences, the corresponding IPC sections are replaced by BNS sections. Section 497 IPC is excluded as it is not included in BNS. The rest remains s ame.

content: Subject: Procedure in cases which Magistrate cannot dispose of.. BNSS Section 361 corresponds to CrPC Section 322. Changes: No change.
Subject: Procedure when, after commencement of inquiry or trial, Magistrate finds case should be committed.. BNSS Section 362 corresponds to CrPC Section 323. Changes: No change.
Subject: Trial of persons previously convicted of offences against coinage, stamp-law or property.. BNSS Section 363 corresponds to CrPC Section 324. Changes: No change.
Subject: Procedure when Magistrate cannot pass sentence sufficiently severe.. BNSS Section 364 corresponds to CrPC Section 325. Changes: No change.
Subject: Conviction or commitment on evidence partly recorded by one Magistrate and partly by another.. BNSS Section 365 corresponds to CrPC Section 326. Changes: No change.
Subject: Court to be open.. BNSS Section 366 corresponds to CrPC Section 327. Changes: No change.

Subject: Procedure in case of accused being person of unsound mind.. BNSS Section 367 corresponds to CrPC Section 328. Changes: “person of unsound mine” in the heading replaces “lunatic” Subsection (2) specifies about psychologist to be "of Government hospital or Government medical college" and "mental retardation" is replaced by "intellectual disability".

Subject: Procedure in case of person of unsound mind tried before Court.. BNSS Section 368 corresponds to CrPC Section 329. Changes: Nearest medical college is now specified as Government medical college. "mental retardation" is replaced by "intellectual disability".

Subject: Release of person with mental illness pending investigation or trial.. BNSS Section 369 corresponds to CrPC Section 330. Changes: "mental retardation" is replaced by "intellectual disability" and "public mental health establishment" replaces "lunatic asylum".
Subject: Resumption of inquiry or trial.. BNSS Section 370 corresponds to CrPC Section 331. Changes: No change.
Subject: Procedure on accused appearing before Magistrate or Court.. BNSS Section 371 corresponds to CrPC Section 332. Changes: No change.
Subject: When accused appears to have been of sound mind.. BNSS Section 372 corresponds to CrPC Section 333. Changes: No change.

Subject: Person acquitted on ground of unsoundness of mind to be detained in safe custody.. BNSS Section 374 corresponds to CrPC Section 335. Changes: "public mental health establishment" replaces "lunatic asylum", and "Mental Health care Act, 2017(10 of 2017" replaces "Indian LunacyAct,1912 (4 of 1912)".
Subject: Power of State Government to empower officer-in-charge to discharge.. BNSS Section 375 corresponds to CrPC Section 336. Changes: No change.

Subject: Procedure where prisoner of unsound mind is reported capable of making his defence.. BNSS Section 376 corresponds to CrPC Section 337. Changes: "person of unsound mind" replaces "lunatic", "lunatic asylum, the visitors of such asylum or any two of them," is replaced by "public mental health establishment, the Mental Health Review Board constituted under the Mental Health care Act, 2017(10 of 2017) ".

Subject: Procedure where person of unsound mind detained is declared fit to be released.. BNSS Section 377 corresponds to CrPC Section 338. Changes: The words "person of unsound mind" replace "lunatic" in the heading. "public mental health establishment" replaces "lunatic asylum".

Subject: Delivery of person of unsound mind to care of relative or friend.. BNSS Section 378 corresponds to CrPC Section 339. Changes: The words "person of unsound mind" replace "lunatic" in the heading.
Subject: Procedure in cases mentioned in section 215.. BNSS Section 379 corresponds to CrPC Section 340. Changes: No change.
Subject: Appeal.. BNSS Section 380 corresponds to CrPC Section 341. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power to order costs.. BNSS Section 381 corresponds to CrPC Section 342. Changes: No change.
Subject: Procedure of Magistrate taking cognizance.. BNSS Section 382 corresponds to CrPC Section 343. Changes: No change.

Subject: Procedure in certain cases of contempt.. BNSS Section 384 corresponds to CrPC Section 345. Changes: BNS sections replace IPC Sections. Fine increased to "one thousand rupees" from "two hundred rupees".
Subject: Procedure where Court considers that case should not be dealt with under section 384.. BNSS Section 385 corresponds to CrPC Section 346. Changes: No change.
Subject: When Registrar or Sub- Registrar to be deemed a Civil Court.. BNSS Section 386 corresponds to CrPC Section 347. Changes: No change.
Subject: Discharge of offender on submission of apology.. BNSS Section 387 corresponds to CrPC Section 348. Changes: No change.
Subject: Imprisonment or committal of person refusing to answer or produce document.. BNSS Section 388 corresponds to CrPC Section 349. Changes: No change.
Subject: Appeals from convictions under sections 383, 384, 388 and 389.. BNSS Section 390 corresponds to CrPC Section 351. Changes: No change.
Subject: Certain Judges and Magistrates not to try certain offences when committed before themselves.. BNSS Section 391 corresponds to CrPC Section 352. Changes: No change.

Subject: Judgment.. BNSS Section 392 corresponds to CrPC Section 353. Changes: "pleaders" is replaced by "advocates." A new proviso is added to subsection (4), which provides that the Court shall, as far as practicable, upload a copy of the judgement on its portal within a period of seven days from the date of judgement. And the words "either in person or through audio-video e lectronic means". are added in subsection (5).
Subject: Language and contents of judgment.. BNSS Section 393 corresponds to CrPC Section 354. Changes: No change.
Subject: Order for notifying address of previously convicted offender.. BNSS Section 394 corresponds to CrPC Section 356. Changes: Reframing of phrases without any change in essence.
Subject: Order to pay compensation.. BNSS Section 395 corresponds to CrPC Section 357. Changes: No change.

Subject: Victim compensation scheme. Compensation to be in addition to fine under section 65, section 70 and sub- section (1) of section 124 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 [under section 326A or section 376D of Indian Penal Code].. BNSS Section 396 corresponds to CrPC Section 357A & 357B. Changes: No change.

Subject: Treatment of victims.. BNSS Section 397 corresponds to CrPC Section 357C. Changes: BNS sections replace corresponding IPC sections. "under sections 4, 6, 8 or section 10 of the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012 " is added.

Subject: Witness protection scheme.. BNSS Section 398 is a new provision with no equivalent in the CrPC. Changes: “Every State Government shall prepare and notify a Witness Protection Scheme for the State with a view to ensure protection of the witnesses”.
Subject: Compensation to persons groundlessly arrested.. BNSS Section 399 corresponds to CrPC Section 358. Changes: No change.
Subject: Order to pay costs in non- cognizable cases.. BNSS Section 400 corresponds to CrPC Section 359. Changes: "pleader" is replaced by "advocate".

Subject: Special reasons to be recorded in certain cases.. BNSS Section 402 corresponds to CrPC Section 361. Changes: ‘The Children Act 1960 (60 of 1960)" is replaced by "The Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015".
Subject: Court not to alter judgment.. BNSS Section 403 corresponds to CrPC Section 362. Changes: No change.

Subject: Copy of judgment to be given to the accused and other persons.. BNSS Section 404 corresponds to CrPC Section 363. Changes: New Proviso added to subsection (5), which provides further that the Court may, on an application made on this behalf by the Prosecuting Officer, provide to the Government, free of cost, a certified copy of such judgement, order, deposition, or record.
Subject: Judgment when to be translated. BNSS Section 405 corresponds to CrPC Section 364. Changes: The words "the accused so required" replaced by "if either party so requires".
Subject: Court of Session to send copy of finding and sentence to District Magistrate.. BNSS Section 406 corresponds to CrPC Section 365. Changes: No change.
Subject: Sentence of death to be submitted by Court of Session for confirmation.. BNSS Section 407 corresponds to CrPC Section 366. Changes: The words "shall be" replaced by "forthwith".
Subject: Power to direct further inquiry to be made or additional evidence to be taken.. BNSS Section 408 corresponds to CrPC Section 367. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power of High Court to confirm sentence or annul conviction.. BNSS Section 409 corresponds to CrPC Section 368. Changes: No change.
Subject: Confirmation or new sentence to be signed by two Judges.. BNSS Section 410 corresponds to CrPC Section 369. Changes: No change.
Subject: Procedure in case of difference of opinion.. BNSS Section 411 corresponds to CrPC Section 370. Changes: No change.
Subject: Procedure in cases submitted to High Court for confirmation.. BNSS Section 412 corresponds to CrPC Section 371. Changes: The words "either physically, or through electronic means" is added.
Subject: No appeal to lie unless otherwise provided.. BNSS Section 413 corresponds to CrPC Section 372. Changes: No change.
Subject: Appeal from orders requiring security or refusal to accept or rejecting surety for keeping peace or good behaviour.. BNSS Section 414 corresponds to CrPC Section 373. Changes: No change.
Subject: No appeal in certain cases when accused pleads guilty.. BNSS Section 416 corresponds to CrPC Section 375. Changes: "Metropolitan Magistrate" excluded.
Subject: No appeal in petty cases.. BNSS Section 417 corresponds to CrPC Section 376. Changes: "Metropolitan Magistrate" excluded. Imprisonment limit is reduced from six months to three months.

Subject: Appeal by the State Government against sentence.. BNSS Section 418 corresponds to CrPC Section 377. Changes: The words "the Delhi Special Police Establishment, constituted under the Delhi Special Police Establishment Act, 1946 (25 of 1946), or by any other" are excluded.
Subject: Appeal in case of acquittal.. BNSS Section 419 corresponds to CrPC Section 378. Changes: Ditto.
Subject: Appeal against conviction by High Court in certain cases.. BNSS Section 420 corresponds to CrPC Section 379. Changes: No change.
Subject: Special right of appeal in certain cases.. BNSS Section 421 corresponds to CrPC Section 380. Changes: The word "contained" excluded.
Subject: Appeal to Court of Session how heard.. BNSS Section 422 corresponds to CrPC Section 381. Changes: The word "Assistant Session Judge" excluded.
Subject: Petition of appeal.. BNSS Section 423 corresponds to CrPC Section 382. Changes: "pleader" is replaced by "advocate".
Subject: Procedure when appellant in jail.. BNSS Section 424 corresponds to CrPC Section 383. Changes: No change.
Subject: Procedure for hearing appeals not dismissed summarily.. BNSS Section 426 corresponds to CrPC Section 385. Changes: Ditto.
Subject: Powers of the Appellate Court.. BNSS Section 427 corresponds to CrPC Section 386. Changes: Ditto.
Subject: Judgments of Subordinate Appellate Court.. BNSS Section 428 corresponds to CrPC Section 387. Changes: No change.
Subject: Order of High Court on appeal to be certified to lower Court.. BNSS Section 429 corresponds to CrPC Section 388. Changes: No change.
Subject: Suspension of sentence pending the appeal; release of appellant on bail.. BNSS Section 430 corresponds to CrPC Section 389. Changes: "or bail bond" added.
Subject: Arrest of accused in appeal from acquittal. BNSS Section 431 corresponds to CrPC Section 390. Changes: No change.
Subject: Appellate Court may take further evidence or direct it to be taken.. BNSS Section 432 corresponds to CrPC Section 391. Changes: "pleader" is replaced by "advocate".
Subject: Procedure where Judges of Court of Appeal are equally divided.. BNSS Section 433 corresponds to CrPC Section 392. Changes: No change.
Subject: Abatement of appeals.. BNSS Section 435 corresponds to CrPC Section 394. Changes: No change.
Subject: Reference to High Court.. BNSS Section 436 corresponds to CrPC Section 395. Changes: "Metropolitan Magistrate" and “or him” excluded.
Subject: Disposal of case according to decision of High Court.. BNSS Section 437 corresponds to CrPC Section 396. Changes: No change.
Subject: Calling for records to exercise powers of revision.. BNSS Section 438 corresponds to CrPC Section 397. Changes: The words "or bail bond " added and "bail" excluded.
Subject: Power to order inquiry.. BNSS Section 439 corresponds to CrPC Section 398. Changes: No change.
Subject: Sessions Judge's powers of revision.. BNSS Section 440 corresponds to CrPC Section 399. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power of Additional Sessions Judge.. BNSS Section 441 corresponds to CrPC Section 400. Changes: No change.
Subject: High Court's powers of revision.. BNSS Section 442 corresponds to CrPC Section 401. Changes: "pleader" is replaced by "advocate".
Subject: Power of High Court to withdraw or transfer revision cases.. BNSS Section 443 corresponds to CrPC Section 402. Changes: No change.
Subject: Option of Court to hear parties.. BNSS Section 444 corresponds to CrPC Section 403. Changes: “pleader" is replaced by "advocate".
Subject: High Court's order to be certified to lower Court.. BNSS Section 445 corresponds to CrPC Section 405. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power of Supreme Court to transfer cases and appeals.. BNSS Section 446 corresponds to CrPC Section 406. Changes: "not exceeding one thousand rupees" is excluded.

Subject: Power of High Court to transfer cases and appeals.. BNSS Section 447 corresponds to CrPC Section 407. Changes: In subsection (4) "or bail bond" is added and "with or without sureties" excluded. From the subsection (7) "not exceeding one thousand rupees" is excluded.

Subject: Power of Sessions Judge to transfer cases and appeals.. BNSS Section 448 corresponds to CrPC Section 408. Changes: The words "one thousand rupees" are replaced by “sum”, whereas "two hundred and fifty rupees" is replaced by “sum not exceeding ten thousand rupees".
Subject: Withdrawal of cases and appeals by Session Judge.. BNSS Section 449 corresponds to CrPC Section 409. Changes: From subsection (1) "any Assistant Sessions Judge or" are excluded.
Subject: Withdrawal of cases by Judicial Magistrate.. BNSS Section 450 corresponds to CrPC Section 410. Changes: No change.
Subject: Making over or withdrawal of cases by Executive Magistrates.. BNSS Section 451 corresponds to CrPC Section 411. Changes: No change.
Subject: Reasons to be recorded.. BNSS Section 452 corresponds to CrPC Section 412. Changes: No change.
Subject: Execution of order passed under section 409.. BNSS Section 453 corresponds to CrPC Section 413. Changes: No change.
Subject: Execution of sentence of death passed by High Court.. BNSS Section 454 corresponds to CrPC Section 414. Changes: No change.
BNSS Section The words "one thousand rupees" are replaced by “sum”, is a new provision with no equivalent in the CrPC.
