content: Subject: Postponement of capital sentence on pregnant woman.. BNSS Section 456 corresponds to CrPC Section 416. Changes: The heading "Commutation of sentence of death on pregnant woman" replaces the heading "Postponement of capital sentence on pregnant woman".

Subject: Power to appoint place of imprisonment.. BNSS Section 457 corresponds to CrPC Section 417. Changes: The words "or section 23 of the Provincial Insolvency Act, 1920 (5 of 1920), as the case may be;" excluded.
Subject: Execution of sentence of imprisonment.. BNSS Section 458 corresponds to CrPC Section 418. Changes: No change.
Subject: Direction of warrant for execution.. BNSS Section 459 corresponds to CrPC Section 419. Changes: No change.
Subject: Warrant with whom to be lodged.. BNSS Section 460 corresponds to CrPC Section 420. Changes: No change.
Subject: Warrant for levy of fine.. BNSS Section 461 corresponds to CrPC Section 421. Changes: The words, "but no such payment has been made" added in subsection (1).
Subject: Effect of such warrant.. BNSS Section 462 corresponds to CrPC Section 422. Changes: No change.
Subject: Warrant for levy of fine issued by a Court in any territory to which this Sanhita does not extend.. BNSS Section 463 corresponds to CrPC Section 423. Changes: No change.

Subject: Suspension of execution of sentence of imprisonment.. BNSS Section 464 corresponds to CrPC Section 424. Changes: The words "with or without sureties" are excluded" and or bail bond" are added.
Subject: Who may issue warrant.. BNSS Section 465 corresponds to CrPC Section 425. Changes: No change.
Subject: Sentence on escaped convict when to take effect.. BNSS Section 466 corresponds to CrPC Section 426. Changes: No change.
Subject: Sentence on offender already sentenced for another offence.. BNSS Section 467 corresponds to CrPC Section 427. Changes: No change.
Subject: Period of detention undergone by the accused to be set off against the sentence of imprisonment.. BNSS Section 468 corresponds to CrPC Section 428. Changes: No change.
Subject: Saving.. BNSS Section 469 corresponds to CrPC Section 429. Changes: No change.
Subject: Return of warrant on execution of sentence.. BNSS Section 470 corresponds to CrPC Section 430. Changes: No change.
Subject: Money ordered to be paid recoverable as a fine.. BNSS Section 471 corresponds to CrPC Section 431. Changes: No change.
Subject: Mercy Petition in death sentence cases.. BNSS Section 472 is a new provision with no equivalent in the CrPC. Changes: Newly added section.

Subject: Power to commute sentence.. BNSS Section 474 corresponds to CrPC Section 433. Changes: Five new clauses are added regarding commutation of sentence and four clauses are excluded from 433 CrPC.
Subject: Restriction on powers of remission or commutation in certain cases.. BNSS Section 475 corresponds to CrPC Section 433A. Changes: No change.
Subject: Concurrent power of Central Government in case of death sentences.. BNSS Section 476 corresponds to CrPC Section 434. Changes: No change.

Subject: State Government to act after concurrence with Central Government in certain cases.. BNSS Section 477 corresponds to CrPC Section 435. Changes: The word "concurrence" replaces "consultation". The words "the Delhi Special Police Establishment constituted under the Delhi Special Police Establishment Act, 1946 (25 of 1946), or" are excluded.

Subject: In what cases bail to be taken.. BNSS Section 478 corresponds to CrPC Section 436. Changes: The word “bail” is replaced by "or bail bond". “without sureties” is excluded. In subsection(2) “bond or”, “or bail bond” are added.

Subject: Maximum period for which an undertrial prisoner can be detained.. BNSS Section 479 corresponds to CrPC Section 436A. Changes: “or life imprisonment is added." One new proviso is added to subsection (1), which states about the bail of first-time offenders. Two new subsections (2) and (3) are added, which are about bail for individuals having multiple pending investigations, inquiries or trials for more than one offence, and applications by the superintendent of jail where the accused person is detained, respectively.“bail” is added in place of “his personal bond with or without sureties," “also” is used in place of “further”.

Subject: When bail may be taken in case of non-bailable offence.. BNSS Section 480 corresponds to CrPC Section 437. Changes: In first proviso "is under the age of 16 years" is replaced by "child". In third proviso, "or for police custody beyond the first fifteen days" added. The words "without sureties" are excluded.

Subject: Bail to require accused to appear before next appellate Court.. BNSS Section 481 corresponds to CrPC Section 437A. Changes: The words "bail bond with sureties" are replaced by "bond or bail bond".
Subject: Direction for grant of bail to person apprehending arrest.. BNSS Section 482 corresponds to CrPC Section 438. Changes: No change.
Subject: Special powers of High Court or Court of Session regarding bail.. BNSS Section 483 corresponds to CrPC Section 439. Changes: No change.
Subject: Amount of bond and reduction thereof.. BNSS Section 484 corresponds to CrPC Section 440. Changes: No change.

Subject: Bond of accused and sureties.. BNSS Section 485 corresponds to CrPC Section 441. Changes: The words "bail or released on his own bond" replaced by "bond or bail bond" and “bail” is replaced by “bond or bail
Subject: Declaration by sureties.. BNSS Section 486 corresponds to CrPC Section 441A. Changes: No change.
Subject: Discharge from custody.. BNSS Section 487 corresponds to CrPC Section 442. Changes: The words "bail bond" are added in subsection (1) and (2).
Subject: Power to order sufficient bail when that first taken is insufficient.. BNSS Section 488 corresponds to CrPC Section 443. Changes: No change.
Subject: Discharge of sureties.. BNSS Section 489 corresponds to CrPC Section 444. Changes: No change.
Subject: Deposit instead of recognizance.. BNSS Section 490 corresponds to CrPC Section 445. Changes: The words "bond with or without sureties" are replaced by "bond or bail bond".
Subject: Procedure when bond has been forfeited.. BNSS Section 491 corresponds to CrPC Section 446. Changes: No change.
Subject: Cancellation of bond and bail bond.. BNSS Section 492 corresponds to CrPC Section 446A. Changes: The words "bail bond" are added.
Subject: Procedure in case of insolvency of death of surety or when a bond is forfeited.. BNSS Section 493 corresponds to CrPC Section 447. Changes: No change.
Subject: Bond required from child.. BNSS Section 494 corresponds to CrPC Section 448. Changes: The word "minor" is replaced by "child".
Subject: Appeal from orders under section 491.. BNSS Section 495 corresponds to CrPC Section 449. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power to direct levy of amount due on certain recognizances.. BNSS Section 496 corresponds to CrPC Section 450. Changes: No change.
Subject: Order for custody and disposal of property pending trial in certain cases.. BNSS Section 497 corresponds to CrPC Section 451.

Subject: Order for disposal of property at conclusion of trial.. BNSS Section 498 corresponds to CrPC Section 452. Changes: The words "investigation" and "or the Magistrate” are added, and “Court” is replaced by “case” in subsection (1). The words “or the Magistrate” is added in subsection (2).

Subject: Payment to innocent purchaser of money found on accused.. BNSS Section 499 corresponds to CrPC Section 453. Changes: The words "within six months from the date of such order" are added for specifying the time limit for delivery to the purchaser.
Subject: Appeal against orders under section 498 or section 499.. BNSS Section 500 corresponds to CrPC Section 454. Changes: The word “or Magistrate” is added.
Subject: Destruction of libellous and other matter.. BNSS Section 501 corresponds to CrPC Section 455. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power to restore possession of immovable property.. BNSS Section 502 corresponds to CrPC Section 456. Changes: The words "attended by" are replaced by "by use of" in subsection (1).

Subject: Procedure where no claimant appears within six months.. BNSS Section 504 corresponds to CrPC Section 458. Changes: The words "may be prescribed" are replaced by "as the State Government may, by rules, provide".
Subject: Power to sell perishable property.. BNSS Section 505 corresponds to CrPC Section 459. Changes: "five hundred rupees" is replaced by "less than ten thousand rupees".
Subject: Irregularities which do not vitiate proceedings.. BNSS Section 506 corresponds to CrPC Section 460. Changes: No change.
Subject: Irregularities which vitiate proceedings.. BNSS Section 507 corresponds to CrPC Section 461. Changes: The words "or telegraph" are excluded.
Subject: Proceedings in wrong place.. BNSS Section 508 corresponds to CrPC Section 462. Changes: No change.
Subject: Non-compliance with provisions of section 183 or section 316.. BNSS Section 509 corresponds to CrPC Section 463. Changes: No change.
Subject: Effect of omission to frame, or absence of, or error in, charge.. BNSS Section 510 corresponds to CrPC Section 464. Changes: No change.
Subject: Finding or sentence when reversible by reason of error, omission or irregularity.. BNSS Section 511 corresponds to CrPC Section 465. Changes: No change.
Subject: Defect or error not to make attachment unlawful.. BNSS Section 512 corresponds to CrPC Section 466. Changes: Ditto.
Subject: Definitions.. BNSS Section 513 corresponds to CrPC Section 467. Changes: No change.

Subject: Bar to taking cognizance after lapse of the period of limitation.. BNSS Section 514 corresponds to CrPC Section 468. Changes: A new explanation is added. "For the purpose of computing the period of limitation, the relevant date shall be the date of filing a complaint under section 223 or the date of recording information under section 173." “elsewhere” is excluded from subsection (1).
Subject: Commencement of the period of limitation.. BNSS Section 515 corresponds to CrPC Section 469. Changes: No change.
Subject: Exclusion of time in certain cases.. BNSS Section 516 corresponds to CrPC Section 470. Changes: No change.
Subject: Exclusion of date on which Court is closed.. BNSS Section 517 corresponds to CrPC Section 471. Changes: No change.
Subject: Continuing offence.. BNSS Section 518 corresponds to CrPC Section 472. Changes: No change.
Subject: Extension of period of limitation in certain cases.. BNSS Section 519 corresponds to CrPC Section 473. Changes: No change.
Subject: Trials before High Courts.. BNSS Section 520 corresponds to CrPC Section 474. Changes: No change.
Subject: Forms.. BNSS Section 522 corresponds to CrPC Section 476. Changes: No change.
Subject: Power of High Court to make rules.. BNSS Section 523 corresponds to CrPC Section 477. Changes: The word "prescribed" is replaced by "provided by rules made by the State Government".
Subject: Power to alter functions allocated to Executive Magistrate in certain cases.. BNSS Section 524 corresponds to CrPC Section 478. Changes: No change.
Subject: Case in which Judge or Magistrate is personally interested.. BNSS Section 525 corresponds to CrPC Section 479. Changes: No change.
Subject: Practicing advocate not to sit as Magistrate in certain Courts.. BNSS Section 526 corresponds to CrPC Section 480. Changes: The word "pleader" is replaced by "advocate".
Subject: Public servant concerned in sale not to purchase or bid for property.. BNSS Section 527 corresponds to CrPC Section 481. Changes: No change.
Subject: Saving of inherent powers of High Court.. BNSS Section 528 corresponds to CrPC Section 482. Changes: Ditto.

Subject: Duty of High Court to exercise continuous superintendence over Courts.. BNSS Section 529 corresponds to CrPC Section 483. Changes: In the heading words "Court of Judicial Magistrates" are replaced by "Courts". And "Courts of Session and", "the Judges and" are added. The word "such" is excluded.

Subject: Trial and proceedings to be held in electronic mode.. BNSS Section 530 is a new provision with no equivalent in the CrPC. Changes: Newly added under heading Trial and proceedings to be held in electronic mode.

Subject: Repeal and savings.. BNSS Section 531 corresponds to CrPC Section 484. Changes: Proviso to subsection (2) (a) is excluded. In sub-section 2(b), the word "prescribed" is replaced at one place by "provided by rules" and at another place by the word “specified”. Proviso to subsection (2) (d) is excluded. In sub-section (3), the word “prescribed” is replaced by “specified”. The word “old” is replaced by “said” at many places in this section.
Subject: "Court".. BSA Section 2(1)(a) corresponds to IEA Section 3, para 1. Changes: No change.
Subject: "disproved".. BSA Section 2(1) (c) corresponds to IEA Section 3, para 8. Changes: Words "A fact is said to be disproved" are replaced by "in relation to a fact, means".

Subject: "document".. BSA Section 2(1)(d) corresponds to IEA Section 3, para 5. Changes: The words "or otherwise recorded" and "or any other means", "and includes electronic and digital records" are added.
Subject: "evidence".. BSA Section 2(1)(e ) corresponds to IEA Section 3, para 6. Changes: "Words" "including statements given electronically" in (e ) (i) and "or digital" in (e ) (ii) are added.
Subject: "facts in issue".. BSA Section 2(1)(g) corresponds to IEA Section 3, para 4. Changes: No change.
Subject: "may presume".. BSA Section 2(1)(h) corresponds to IEA Section 4, para 1. Changes: No change.
Subject: "not proved".. BSA Section 2(1)(i) corresponds to IEA Section 3, para 9. Changes: No change.
Subject: "proved".. BSA Section 2(1)(j) corresponds to IEA Section 3, para 7. Changes: No change.
Subject: "relevant".. BSA Section 2(1)(k) corresponds to IEA Section 3, para 3. Changes: No change.
Subject: "shall presume".. BSA Section 2(1)(l) corresponds to IEA Section 4, para 2. Changes: No change.

Subject: Words and expressions.. BSA Section 2(2) is a new provision with no equivalent in the IEA. Changes: Newly added. “Words and expressions used herein and not defined but defined in the Information Technology Act, 2000 (21 of 2000), the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023, and the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, shall have the same meanings as assigned to them in the said Act and Sanhitas.”
Subject: Relevancy of facts forming part of same transaction.. BSA Section 4 corresponds to IEA Section 6. Changes: No change.
Subject: Facts which are occasion, cause or effect of facts in issue or relevant facts.. BSA Section 5 corresponds to IEA Section 7. Changes: Words "or relevant facts" added in heading.

Subject: Motive, preparation and previous or subsequent conduct.. BSA Section 6 corresponds to IEA Section 8. Changes: In explanation. And in illustrations "vakils" is replaced by "advocates" "man" is replaced by "person" and "ravished" is replaced by " raped".

Subject: Facts necessary to explain or introduce fact in issue or relevant facts.. BSA Section 7 corresponds to IEA Section 9. Changes: Words "fact in issue or" are added in the heading. "A" is replaced by "he".

Subject: Things said or done by conspirator in reference to common design.. BSA Section 8 corresponds to IEA Section 10. Changes: No change except in illustrations, "Government of India" is replaced by "State", and city names are changed from Calcutta to Kolkata, Bombay to Mumbai, and Kabul to Singapore.

Subject: When facts not otherwise relevant become relevant.. BSA Section 9 corresponds to IEA Section 11. Changes: No Change except in illustrations cities names are changed from Calcutta to Chennai and Lahore to Laddakh.

Subject: Facts tending to enable Court to determine amount are relevant in suits for damages.. BSA Section 10 corresponds to IEA Section 12. Changes: No change except in the heading, where the words "In suits for damages" are placed at the end instead of the beginning.
Subject: Facts relevant when right or custom is in question.. BSA Section 11 corresponds to IEA Section 13. Changes: No change.

Subject: Facts showing existence of state of mind, or of body or bodily feeling.. BSA Section 12 corresponds to IEA Section 14. Changes: In illustrations, word "coin" is replaced by "currency" and "carriage" is replaced by "cars".
Subject: Facts bearing on question whether act was accidental or intentional.. BSA Section 13 corresponds to IEA Section 15. Changes: In illustrations, word "rupee" is replaced by "currency".
Subject: Existence of course of business when relevant.. BSA Section 14 corresponds to IEA Section 16. Changes: No change.
Subject: Admission defined.. BSA Section 15 corresponds to IEA Section 17. Changes: No change.
Subject: Admission by party to proceeding or his agent.. BSA Section 16 corresponds to IEA Section 18. Changes: Headings of paragraphs are excluded and written as subsections and clauses.
Subject: Admissions by persons whose position must be proved as against party to suit.. BSA Section 17 corresponds to IEA Section 19. Changes: No change.
Subject: Admissions by persons expressly referred to by party to suit.. BSA Section 18 corresponds to IEA Section 20. Changes: No change.
Subject: Proof of admissions against persons making them, and by or on their behalf.. BSA Section 19 corresponds to IEA Section 21. Changes: Word "coin" is replaced by "currency".
Subject: When oral admissions as to contents of documents are relevant.. BSA Section 20 corresponds to IEA Section 22. Changes: No change.

Subject: Confession made after removal of impression caused by inducement, threat or promise, relevant.. BSA Section First proviso to section 22 corresponds to IEA Section 28. Changes: Heading is dropped as section is included as proviso.

Subject: Confession otherwise relevant not to become irrelevant because of promise of secrecy, etc.. BSA Section Second proviso to section 22 corresponds to IEA Section 29. Changes: Heading is dropped as section is included as proviso.
Subject: Confession to police officer.. BSA Section 23(1) corresponds to IEA Section 25. Changes: Words "not to be proved" are excluded from heading.

Subject: Confession by accused while in custody of police not to be proved against him.. BSA Section 23(2) corresponds to IEA Section 26. Changes: Heading is dropped as the section is included as a subsection. Word "whilst" is replaced by "while" and words "such person" are replaced by "him".

Subject: How much of information received from accused may be proved.. BSA Section Proviso to section 23 corresponds to IEA Section 27. Changes: Heading is dropped as the section is included as proviso.

Subject: Consideration of proved confession affecting person making it and others jointly under trial for same offence.. BSA Section 24 corresponds to IEA Section 30. Changes: A new explanation II is added, mentioning that "A trial of more persons than one held in the absence of the accused who has absconded or who fails to comply with a proclamation issued under Section 84 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023, shall be deemed to be a joint trial for the purpose of this section."
Subject: Admissions not conclusive proof, but may estop.. BSA Section 25 corresponds to IEA Section 31. Changes: No change.

Subject: Cases in which statement of relevant fact by person who is dead or cannot be found, etc., is relevant.. BSA Section 26 corresponds to IEA Section 32. Changes: Word "namely" is added and headings of subsections are dropped. In illustration word "ravished" is replaced by "raped" and word "banya" is replaced by "business".

Subject: Relevancy of certain evidence for proving, in subsequent proceeding, truth of facts therein stated.. BSA Section 27 corresponds to IEA Section 33. Changes: No change except that word "that" is replaced by "and" in proviso.

Subject: Entries in books of account when relevant.. BSA Section 28 corresponds to IEA Section 34. Changes: No change except the words "include those maintained in an electronic form" are excluded from the heading.
Subject: Relevancy of statements in maps, charts and plans.. BSA Section 30 corresponds to IEA Section 36. Changes: No change.

Subject: Relevancy of statement as to fact of public nature contained in certain Acts or notifications.. BSA Section 31 corresponds to IEA Section 37. Changes: Words "any Act of Parliament 4 (of the United Kingdom) or or in any 5 Central Act, Provincial Act or 6 a State Act or in a Government notification or notification by the Crown Representative appearing in the Official Gazette or in any printed paper purporting to be the London Gazette or the Government Gazette of any Dominion, colony, or possession of his Majesty is a relevant fact." are replaced by "any Central Act or State Act or in a Central Government or State Government notification appearing in the respective Official Gazette or in any printed paper or in electronic or digital form purporting to be such Gazette, is a r elevant fact".

Subject: Relevancy of statements as to any law contained in law books including electronic or digital form.. BSA Section 32 corresponds to IEA Section 38. Changes: Words "including in electronic or digital form" are added in heading as well as in the main text of the section.

Subject: What evidence to be given when statement forms part of a conversation, document, electronic record, book or series of letters or papers.. BSA Section 33 corresponds to IEA Section 39. Changes: No change.
Subject: Previous judgments relevant to bar a second suit or trial.. BSA Section 34 corresponds to IEA Section 40. Changes: No change.

Subject: Relevancy of certain judgments in probate, etc., jurisdiction.. BSA Section 35 corresponds to IEA Section 41. Changes: The word "Tribunal" is added. Paragraph "Such judgment, order or decree is conclusive proof that" is given as subsection (2) and conditions under this are enumerated as (i), (ii), (iii), and (iv). The word "that" is excluded.
Subject: Relevancy and effect of judgments, orders or decrees, other than those mentioned in section35[41 IEA].. BSA Section 36 corresponds to IEA Section 42. Changes: No change.
Subject: Judgments, etc., other than those mentioned in sections 34, 35 and 36 [40to 42 IEA] when relevant.. BSA Section 37 corresponds to IEA Section 43. Changes: No change.
Subject: Fraud or collusion in obtaining judgment, or incompetency of Court, may be proved.. BSA Section 38 corresponds to IEA Section 44. Changes: No change.
